week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 9–1 blowout with 8 HR, 38 K, and three contributors (Abreu, Schanuel, Baldwin) each going deep — the most complete team in the league through four weeks | same
2. BaseballPro77 | Trout's 3-HR, .667 OBP explosion and Murakami's 3 HR made this an 8–2 rout; consistent elite production in all three phases | up
3. Athletic Park Dragons | Survived a near-upset with a 5–4 squeaker; Gleyber Torres and Andrés Giménez were excellent, but that ERA needs cleaning up | down
4. North Correa | Chris Sale's 15 K, 0.75 WHIP masterclass anchored a 7–3 win; Arozarena, Betts providing steady offense — quietly the most balanced staff in the league | up
5. Benchwarmers | Zach Neto's 7-run, 2-HR week is a revelation and the 7–3 win is real, but 0 quality starts and a 4.50 ERA will catch up to them against tougher opponents | up
6. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 12-K, 0.33 WHIP gem carried a 6–4 win; Yelich and Turang contributing — this is a team trending the right direction after waiver activity | up
7. General Hospital | Tied 4–4 despite posting a .414 OBP, 23 RBI, and 3 QS — this roster is legitimately good and the tie is a fluke against a scrappy Wonderdogs team | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | The 5–4 loss to the Dragons is a gut-punch considering they dominated pitching categories; McLean's 15 Ks show real ceiling | same
9. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Tied 4–4 in a game they probably deserved to lose — 3 QS and a 2.98 ERA are genuine strengths but the offense remains structurally thin | up
10. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 6–4 but posted 6 HR, 5 SB, and Yordan Alvarez and Jonathan Aranda are real weapons; adding Merrill Kelly is a smart pitching fix | same
11. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 7–3 but Bryan Woo's 0.83 WHIP and 5 NSVH show this team can win pitching weeks — Juan Soto is criminally underperforming so far | down
12. Buttville Skidmarks | Chase DeLauter's 4-HR week and Gausman's 0.17 WHIP are stars on a 3–7 team — the talent is there but the surrounding roster is too inconsistent | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease was excellent and still lost 8–2; zero home runs as a team is a death sentence in this format and the lineup has no answer | same
14. Riverside Roughnecks | A 1–9 loss with a 7.91 ERA and 0 quality starts is as bad as it gets; Shea Langeliers and Royce Lewis provide some hope but the pitching is a disaster | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8–2 week, elite 2.11 ERA, Max Muncy launching HR, deepest all-around roster through 3 weeks | up
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood and Contreras were unstoppable; 40 R and .412 OBP is a league statement | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5-HR explosion anchored a solid 5–3 win; consistent across all three weeks | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Crushed Canberra 8–2 with 64 hits and 9 SB; Bryce Harper's .640 OBP a weapon | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 but posted a 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP — pitching is elite; offense needs to wake up | down
6. North Correa | Lost the matchup but 11 SB and Ohtani's 3 HR show real firepower; speed category dominance is a threat | same
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8–2 but pitching was solid with 75 Ks and 3.99 ERA; hitting depth is the concern | down
8. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is must-start elite, but tying General Hospital when you should've won is a missed opportunity | same
9. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Pitching is genuinely strong — 1.13 WHIP, 7 NSVH — but hitting is a serious structural problem | up
10. General Hospital | Kumar Rocker add is intriguing; 70 Ks in a tie stings, but the pitching foundation is real | down
11. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost 5–4 in a close one; Brice Turang's .474 OBP is a bright spot but pitching remains shaky at 5.05 ERA | same
12. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 4 HR and they still got steamrolled 8–2; pitching is simply not good enough | down
13. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 on the back of Trevor Story's 9 RBI but 0 quality starts and a 5.04 ERA is unsustainable | same
14. Benchwarmers | The 6–3 win flatters a team with a 5.53 ERA and 34 Ks; luck index confirms they should be much lower | same
